description: Railway CLI 4.56: 'railway add --database redis' without --json prints the interactive TUI prompt ('What do you need? Database') and appears to hang, but the service IS created. Retrying the apparently-failed command created 5 duplicate Redis services, all billable.
resolution: Always pass --json to railway add/domain/variables — it forces non-interactive output and returns the created serviceId. Before retrying any railway create command that appears to hang, run 'railway status --json' and check whether the resource already exists. Delete strays with 'railway service delete --service <name> --yes'.

description: First Ovyro Railway deploy failed on BOTH web and worker: 'Node.js 18.x has reached End-Of-Life and has been removed'. Nixpacks defaulted to Node 18 because railway.json pinned no version and the repo has no engines field or .nvmrc, despite the project using bun.
resolution: Set NIXPACKS_NODE_VERSION=22 as a service variable on every service. Permanent fix is an engines.node field in package.json so the pin lives in code rather than Railway config.

description: Ovyro worker service deployed as a second copy of the WEB app — it ran 'next start' and web's preDeployCommand instead of 'bun run worker'. Setting RAILWAY_CONFIG_FILE=railway.worker.json as a service ENV VAR silently did nothing; Railway treats config-as-code path as a service SETTING, not an environment variable. Only caught by reading worker runtime logs — deploy status said SUCCESS.
resolution: Set it through the API: POST https://backboard.railway.com/graphql/v2 with mutation serviceInstanceUpdate(serviceId,environmentId,input:{startCommand,railwayConfigFile}). Then redeploy. ALWAYS verify a multi-service deploy by reading each service's runtime logs — a service running the wrong entrypoint still reports SUCCESS.

name: p1-integration-verify-real-stack
description: Phase gate verification of a multi-wave merge: run full gates on merged main (bun build compiles all routes together = the real cross-module check), apply migrations to a FRESH local Postgres (brew postgresql@16, initdb into a gitignored .pg/, TCP-only on :5433), seed admin, boot 'bun run worker' as a separate process to confirm the registry, then drive the lifecycle over real HTTP with Better-Auth session cookies (scripts/verify-lifecycle.ts) + presign validation (scripts/verify-media.ts). External-credential steps (R2 presign, Resend delivery) are reported blocked, never faked; a listing_media row is injected at the DB level only to clear the >=1-photo submit gate.
